rulings.Case Title: THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC., A VS. TONY D. CARRACCI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption; Ntc Of Oppos To Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2013, Line 1: PLAINTIFF THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC.'S Hearing Claim Of Exemption; Notice Of Opposition To Claim Of Exemption Hearing required. The Court is inclined to deny the claim of exemption because Claimant has not demonstrated that the withheld funds from San Francisco Fire Credit Union are necessary for the support of the judgment debtor and his spouse and dependents. (CCP § 703.530(a).) Plaintiff's monthly take-home pay exceeds his total monthly expenses. (Financial Stmt., 2d, 4n.) =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: TOTAL OUTDOOR CORP., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. POSTING ACCESS REVENUE, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Kevin Hick'S Motion To Quash The Deposition Subpoena Of Diana Hicks, Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, December 12, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT KEVIN HICK'S Motion To Quash The Deposition Subpoena Of Diana Hicks, Or, In The Alternative, Motion For Protective Order; And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Kevin Hick's motion for a protective order is denied. Diana Hicks should testify as to matters associated with her role as an employee of the two companies at issue. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2013, Line 9: DEFENDANT SHERRI KING'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND The Court grants cross-complainant leave to file a second amended cross-complaint. Cross-complainant has leave to add a cause of action for breach of implied/quasi contract and a cause/s of action for intentional/negligent interference with prospective advantage/contractual relations. Paragraph 64 of the second amended cross-complaint shall clarify the contractual basis for recovering litigation expenses. The second amended cross-complaint shall remove the tort of another cause of action, which was already the subject of the Court's order dated August 5, 2013 sustaining a demurrer to that cause of action without leave to amend. Cross-defendant may respond to the second amended cross-complaint by demurrer, motion to strike, or answer. rulings.Case Title: BRAYTON PURCELL LLP et al VS. DAVID J. COOK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2013, Line 10: PLAINTIFFS BRAYTON PURCELL LLP'S, JOSEPH GARZA'S, and JESSICA HELLER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT Overruled. A demurrer can be used only to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading under attack or judicially noticeable matters. (Blank v. Kirwin (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Therefore, the Court did not consider the Declaration of David J. Cook or the attached exhibit, or any references to documents not attached to or referenced in the First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") in ruling on this demurrer. As to the failure to state a claim because of violation of Rule 2-200, the FACC does not plead that Cook entered into a fee-splitting agreement with Brayton Purcell. Rather, the FACC alleges that the Garzas hired Cook directly as independent collection counsel for a 25% contingency of the total recovery. (See e.g., FACC, 7, 14.) As to the uncertainty argument, the FACC is not written in a way in which the Cross-Defendant cannot reasonably respond. (Khoury v. Maly's of Calif., Inc. (1993) Cal.App.4th 612, 616.) As to the failure to specify whether the contract was written or oral, the FACC sufficiently alleges facts establishing that Cross-Complainant is alleging a breach of the written agreement attached as Exhibit B to the FACC. The FACC alleges that BP had the authority to enter into the agreement on behalf of the Garzas (FACC, 7), the FACC alleges that BP signed the agreement (FACC, 12), and the prayer for relief seeks as damages the amount set forth in Exhibit B. (FACC, p. 11; 19.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC VS. JOSEPH M. ALIOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2013, Line 14: PLAINTIFF LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE The application of Christopher T. Leahy is granted for good cause shown. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: KAYLEE ANN ULLOM VS. ANOTHER PLANET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2013, Line 15: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff states a claim for dangerous condition of public property under Gov't § 835. Plaintiff alleges that the concert venue was in a defective condition for including a secluded and wooded area known for high crime within the venue's boundaries. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM

rulings.Case Title: KAYLEE ANN ULLOM VS. ANOTHER PLANET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, December 12, 2013, Line 15: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The motion to strike is granted without leave to amend as to items 1 and 6. Those items are irrelevant to the dangerous condition of public property cause of action. The motion is denied as to items 2 through 5, and 7 through 13. The Court cannot state at this point that the allegations are irrelevant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
